content.text: I have route data objects, and I need a function to generate a meaningful display name for each path so it's easy for users to understand.

This function starts by checking if the route has distinct 'from' and 'to' points in its tags. If not, it attempts to build a descriptive name using the available short or alternative names before falling back to the numeric ID.

```python
def create_route_long_name(relation, short_name):
    """Create a meaningful route name."""
    if relation.tags.get('from') and relation.tags.get('to'):
        return "{0}-to-{1}".format(relation.tags.get('from'),
                                   relation.tags.get('to'))
    name = relation.tags.get('name') or\
        relation.tags.get('alt_name') or\
        "OSM Route No. {}".format(relation.id)

    # Drop route_short_name from this one if it contains it
    if short_name and name.startswith(short_name):
        # Drop it
        return name[len(short_name):]
    return name
```

It handles cases where an input prefix might match the resulting full name by stripping that prefix off. This is useful for cleaning up processed route data and ensuring uniqueness.
If a route has no 'name' or 'alt_name', but only a numerical ID, what will happen if that ID happens to start with an existing short name?

The function will first construct the default name using the ID (`OSM Route No. {ID}`). If that generated default name starts with `short_name`, it will strip the prefix from the beginning of the full string.
That clarifies how the naming convention works, thank you.
